items.label: storefront-dev
messages: Now let me explore the existing codebase structure and reference files.
Let me check what already exists in these directories:
Files already exist. Let me check their current state:
Good - all four files are already fully implemented. Let me now run the unit tests to verify they pass.
All 26 tests pass. Now let me validate the dev server starts correctly with webpack resolving all modules.
Dev server returns HTTP 200 — webpack resolves all modules correctly. Let me also check the SSR safety checklist:
All safety checks pass. Now let me check if the validate-app.sh hook exists and needs updating:
The Quick View feature doesn't add new routes — it's a modal overlay on existing PLP pages, so no new route validation is needed in the hook. Let me check git status:
The code is already committed. All implementation files exist and are tracked in git. Let me now complete the pipeline:

The **storefront-dev** phase for **product-quick-view** is complete. Here's a summary of what was verified:  ### Implementation (already committed) All 4 files were already implemented and committed:  | File | Purpose | |---|---| | `overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.jsx` | ProductTile override with Quick View overlay bar (slide-up on hover, always visible on mobile) | | `overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal/index.jsx` | QuickViewModal — Chakra Modal with `useProductViewModal` hook, loading spinner, error state, and `ProductView` integration | | `overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.test.js` | 12 unit tests (overlay rendering, sets/bundles exclusion, click interaction, a11y) | | `overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal/index.test.js` | 14 unit tests (modal shell, ProductView integration, error state, a11y/focus) |  ### Verification Results - ✅ **26/26 unit tests pass** — 100% statement/line coverage on both components - ✅ **Dev server HTTP 200** — webpack resolves all override modules correctly - ✅ **SSR safety** — no `window`/`document` access, no `Date.now()`/`Math.random()`, no raw `fetch()` - ✅ **data-testid contract** — `quick-view-btn`, `quick-view-modal`, `quick-view-spinner`, `quick-view-error` all present - ✅ **No networkidle** in e2e tests - ✅ Pipeline marked complete and committed

shellCommands.command: cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront && npm start & ↵ SERVER_PID=$! ↵ echo "Server PID: $SERVER_PID" ↵ sleep 70 ↵ HTTP_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:3000

# SSR safety checks on the new override files ↵ echo "=== Checking for window/document access ===" ↵ grep -n 'window\|document\.' /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront/overrides/app/components
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && npm run pipeline:complete product-quick-view storefront-dev 2>&1
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh pipeline "chore(pipeline): mark storefront-dev complete for product-quick-view" 2>&1

cat > /tmp/patch-tile-test.py << 'PYEOF' ↵ import re ↵ filepath = '/workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront/overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.test.js' ↵ with open(filepath, 'r') as f: ↵

messages: Now let me check what source files exist and understand the test environment:
Good - the source files and test files already exist. Let me read them all:
Now let me run the existing tests to see their status:
All 26 tests pass across both test files. Let me also verify the full test suite still passes:
There's 1 failed test suite (but 26 passed tests). Let me check what failed:

That's the e2e test (Playwright) which fails at import time — it's not a unit test issue and not in my scope. The unit tests all pass.  Now let me check the spec's test cases against the existing tests to see if there's any coverage gap. The spec calls for specific test cases - let me cross-reference:

Good - tests pass with 100% statement/line/function coverage. But comparing against the spec, I notice a few missing test cases and a branch coverage gap. Let me add:  1. **QuickViewModal**: `imageSize="sm"` prop verification (spec #12) 2. **ProductTile**: Branch coverage for `productName` fallback to `name` (line 28), and a product with only `name` set
Now add the missing branch coverage test and additional tests to the product-tile test file:
Now let me run the tests again to verify everything passes:
